title: Reading and completing livestock movement forms
subtitle: The paperwork that has to be right before the animals leave.
excerpt: Every movement of sheep, goats, pigs, or cattle requires completing a paper or electronic movement document. The forms are not complicated once you know what each field is asking. Getting them wrong delays movements, triggers APHA involvement, and in the worst cases results in animals detained at an abattoir or market pending verification.
sourceNotes: APHA, cattle tracing and movement recording, apha.gov.uk. DEFRA, livestock movement recording guidance, gov.uk. British Cattle Movement Service (BCMS) keeper's guide. eAML2 guidance for pig movements.

glossaryTerms.definition: The paper or electronic form required for moving sheep, goats, pigs, or cattle between holdings, to a market, or to slaughter. Different forms apply to different species: AML1/AML2 for pigs (eAML2 online), SCR for sheep and goats, and BCMS passport + movement form for cattle.

content.text: required differ by species. Sheep and goats use the Sheep and Goat Keeper Record (formerly the SCR), which can be paper or submitted to the Sheep and Goats Kept Records (SGKR) online service. Pigs use eAML2 online. Cattle use BCMS passports. This tutorial covers the sheep/goat and pig forms most relevant to a small mixed holding.

Every sheep or goat leaving or arriving at your holding must be recorded in your SGKR register. The record shows: the date, the number of animals moved, your CPH, the destination CPH (or source CPH for arrivals), and the identification method (ear tag number, flock mark). The register must be kept for 3 years and be available to inspect at short notice. Movements to and from showground, veterinary practices, and between holdings you own are all recordable events.

For pigs, the movement licence on eAML2 must be submitted before the animals leave. The key fields: source CPH, destination CPH, number of pigs, their identification (ear tag or tattoo), and the intended date of movement. The system generates a movement reference number that travels with the pigs. The transport documentation (or a printout of the eAML2 submission) must be carried in the vehicle.
Wrong destination CPH: check the exact CPH number before submitting. A swapped digit sends the notice to the wrong keeper.
Wrong animal count: if the form number differs from the number that arrives at the abattoir, the abattoir must report the gap.
Submitting after the animals have moved: eAML2 must be completed before pigs leave. Back-dating a form is a record-keeping offence.

Print or save the eAML2 confirmation for your records; both species records must be retained for at least 3 years. Keep them in a folder by year; an inspector asking for last year's movement records should be able to retrieve them in minutes, not hours.
